GROUP IDEAS / THEMES

HORSES

Faster, stronger, and more efficient than mules

Plan for 4 horses, running on the same schedule as the humans

Daily activity plan: 6–10 hours of travel, allowing rest and grazing, rationing food supply

WHEELS & MATERIALS

Steel noted as both durable and relatively lightweight

Rubber likely unavailable in the time period

Cart reinforced with steel

Terrain challenges (mud, rain) → suggested tarps and wooden frame (lighter but less durable)

MODERN OBJECTS

Battery/generator connected to wheels

Motion of horses could power the generator

Batteries as a backup if horses fail
column10:
SUMMARY OF GROUP DYNAMICS

Ellen emerged as a leader, steering the conversation, summarizing key points, and helping organize roles for the presentation.

Kannan and Vedant focused heavily on technical trade-offs (steel vs. iron, horses vs. mules), with Kannan also bridging explanations to others.

Will took on a structured role, suggesting votes and practical reinforcements like weatherproofing, and whether rubber was available during the 1800s.

Nora contributed unique and creative ideas (sabotage, safety, daily life planning).

Jose thought about external factors and added creativity by linking ideas to modern references (Red Dead Redemption).
